text: SAY: He is God alone: God the eternal! He begetteth not, and He is not begotten; And there is none like unto Him.
author: The Quran
source: Project Gutenberg eBook #2800 (The Koran, trans. J. M. Rodwell)
out_pulls_note: High heat: four terse, hammer-blow clauses that refuse all partners and likeness -- the absoluteness lands before a stranger can place the source.
search_notes: J. M. Rodwell translation, 1861 (Gutenberg #2800, 'Translator: J. M. Rodwell'), used because it is byte-clean PD; Palmer's SBE Quran was the alternate but I verified Rodwell within budget rather than risk a second scripture scan. The whole of Surah CXII ('The Unity') is quoted verbatim (112:1-4); substring confirmed after whitespace/quote normalisation; no italic underscores. SCRIPTURE RISK NOTED: Rodwell renders v.2 'God the eternal!' for al-Samad, where Palmer/most moderns give 'the Eternal, the Absolute' / 'the Self-Sufficient'; this is a recognised translation choice of a famously hard word, not a misattribution or error -- flagged for transparency. UNSETTLED concept: presented as one tradition's scriptural statement of divine oneness, not as a proof or final answer. Confidence high on text and rights.
